WTI.data_anomaly: price_source_conflict: fast_info prev_close $100.53 vs history $95.08 diverge by 5.5%. Signal suppressed.

macro_summary: US equities set new highs Wednesday as SPY added 1.02% to 733.83 and QQQ gained 1.24% to 695.77, extending the record rally. Hormuz de‑escalation pushed WTI down another 4.9% over the week to $95.72, while SOXX surged 2.39% to an all‑time high 506.87 on AMD’s blowout Q1. New US tariffs on Chinese chips announced — rates unknown, effective June 2027 — add long‑dated supply‑chain uncertainty. Markets eye the FOMC rate decision later today for any shift in the pause narrative.
commodities_note: WTI rose 0.7% to $95.72, but the 5‑day decline of 8.9% reflects deepening Hormuz de-escalation. WTI price sources diverge by 5.5%; using the 5‑day decline as the reliable trend indicator. Gold added 1.1% to $4,705.50; US10Y eased 6bp to 4.36%, and the VIX again ticked lower to 17.39.

singapore.summary: Banks remain in a holding pattern with US10Y at 4.36% — still above the 4.20% NIM‑relief threshold. DBS is hard‑blocked on RSI; UOB (earnings today) and OCBC (tomorrow) are the immediate catalysts that will dictate near‑term sentiment.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — dual NIM compression persists, awaiting catalysts.
singapore.key_development: OCBC’s May 8 print will test whether HSBC Indonesia integration can offset rate headwinds.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX at a fresh all‑time high, but 18 of 21 watchlist semis are on CAUTION — the rally has moved into extreme overbought territory. AMD’s blowout Q1 ignited the latest leg, but NVDA, MU, TSM, and others are now 10‑70% above their moving averages; only TSEM (earnings May 13) and CRWV (tomorrow) offer near‑term binary events that could reset levels.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extended — no fresh entries.
semiconductors.key_development: US‑China chip tariffs (2027 implementation) add long‑dated uncertainty but do not alter the current AI‑demand trajectory.

big_tech.summary: Amazon and Google are severely extended (+20‑24% above SMA50) while Microsoft remains range‑bound near its SMA50 with an OpenAI overhang. Palantir’s premium valuation keeps it on CAUTION; no BigTech name offers a near‑term entry.
big_tech.thesis_status: Structurally bullish but technically overextended — wait for pullbacks.
big_tech.key_development: Amazon Supply Chain Services launch could be a long‑term margin driver, but is not enough to offset the extreme extension.

interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE earnings recovery confirms AI‑optics momentum; COHR recent in‑line print did not stop the Lumentum‑led rally, so wait for better correlation breakdown.

interconnected.entry_note: Consider if AMD MI450 shipment news in H2 2026 signals Meta capex upside beyond $70B.

active_risks: Iran‑US Hormuz de‑escalation holds — WTI down 8.9% on week, supply‑disruption premium fading.
US announces new tariffs on Chinese chips and electronics, effective 2027 — delayed implementation adds supply‑chain uncertainty.
FOMC rate decision today — markets price no change, but hawkish hold could reverse recent tech easing.
MAS S$NEER appreciation still in effect (next decision Oct 2026) — SGD strong, compressing bank NIMs.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil’s retreat has removed the immediate margin‑compression threat, fueling the SPY and SOXX rally. The new China chip tariffs are a 2027 issue, but they reinforce the structural decoupling narrative that benefits INTC and TSMC’s on‑shoring. The FOMC today could inject volatility if the Fed signals a later‑than‑expected pause.
